published.text:
### Hero
Heading: AI Automation for Auto Repair Shops
Body: Opens with shop floor scenario — phone rings while every tech is under a car, call goes to voicemail, customer calls the shop down the street. Frames automation around maintenance reminders, estimate approvals, and the lifetime value of a maintenance customer ($1,500-$3,000/year).
Sub: Below: what auto repair shop automation looks like in practice, where it delivers the clearest ROI, and how to decide if it is worth the investment.

### Core sections
- What you can automate (cards: missed call text-back, appointment scheduling, estimate follow-up, maintenance reminders, client reactivation, review requests)
- Is this a good fit? (evaluation: miss calls on shop floor, unapproved estimates, thousands of past customers with no outreach, 3+ bays vs solo mechanic)
- ROI expectations (considerations: $300-$800 average repair order, maintenance customer LTV, missed call opportunity cost, estimate approval improvement)
- Results from similar service businesses (case studies with auto repair framing)

### FAQ
Subhead: Practical answers for auto repair shops considering automation
5 questions covering most impactful automation, maintenance scheduling, estimate approval workflow, cost, shop software compatibility

### Final CTA
Heading: Stop losing repair jobs to missed calls and unapproved estimates

edits.description: Published as part of batch 5. Targets auto repair shops losing revenue to unanswered calls and dormant customer databases.
Interlinks to HVAC companies, roofing companies, AI phone answering, quote follow-up.
Maintenance reminder angle is the strongest long-term revenue play.
